channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 120 replies — 480.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 120 • Updated: 2026-07-01 08:41 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423389
>>62423360
Why are you even reading this "gaslighting"? Who's forcing you to be here?

62423462
>>62423259
Hilarious deflection after doing this
>>62422274

The absolute state

62423474
>>62423353
How does 2 million a year sound?

62424023
>>62423283
I kept granular details on a project i own for years, to my incomprehensible regret

62424140
>>62423276
The sad fact of the matter is that jeetlinkers will still be delirious off copium a decade from now, even after CLL pulls the plug in five years beforehand.

62424195
good. i never could participate in build anyway as a US fag

62424386
>>62422668
It literally says "the most recent season of Chainlink Rewards will mark the conclusion of Build-related token rewards, with claims ending on July 7, 2026" meaning no season 2+ of build rewards going to stakers.

It also says "Proceeds from these new agreements are expected to be programmatically converted to LINK and used to support the growth of the network through programs such as the Chainlink Reserve." meaning that link goes to the reserve, not to stakers.

Previously
>stakers will get build rewards

Now
>Chainlink gets paid in link

62424528
xswap isnt even on cow swap? where do you sell this trash?

62424728
S E X

62424821
>>62424386
Brutal

62425620
>>62424023
>this project has always been pushed by morons
>also I've been holding this project for years

You're lying. And brown.

>>62424386
You're a brown ESL monkey who doesn't even know what the words "such as" mean.
Protip: the same exact words were used when Build was first introduced.

62425675
>>62417503
HAHAHAHAHAHAHA Another nail in the coffin and shovel full of dirt in the grave on the biggest retail fraud in token history.

62425681
>>62419638
Link is underperforming fucking death spiraled LUNC since Trump revived crypto. Lol And by a lot.

62425687
>>62419707
>A chainlink shill calling someone else brown.

62425699
>>62422159
>>62423276
Perfectly said.

62425962
>>62425620
I'm a 100k link holder. I have more link than you.

"Such as" isn't very fucking reliable. All of this would be fine if link was valued as it should be but it fucking isn't, with no clear path to gaining that value.

62425980
>>62425962
>CLL uses "such as" in 2022 when launching Build
>THIS IS SET IN STONE

>CLL uses "such as" in 2026 when changing Build
>THIS IS NOT VERY FUCKING RELIABLE

You're a brown paid fuddie.

62426004
>>62425980
I'm also whiter than you, Yank.

62426010
>>62425980
>>62425620
post a picture of your hand with timestamp

62426949
i am just getting caught up on this news after the weekend. my my my how the mighty have fallen

i predicted all of this for the last few years but the boyscouts couldn't handle it

>duuuuuuuuuuuude i need a team of tax attorneys for the heckin rewards

BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAH

look at you now. an expensive lesson on controlling your ego. you needed it tho


your hubris was your downfall

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62426967
>>62417512
>There are thousands of other coins.

LOL this dude stuck in 2021. that's your conclusion bro, just buy other scam coins?

don't you people get it? altcoins are dead. capital moved back to actual companies in the stock market. only a retarded sap would continue donating to a bunch of nerds for a "whitepaper"


>>62417833
>I'll also (again just playing the other side) point out the regardless of whether or not it ultimately benefits the LINK token, Chainlink is realistically one of like 5 crypto projects amongst the hundreds of "legitimate" ones that actually is seeing real adoption and has a huge use case that is surviving the infantile stages of crypto, and not just surviving but will thrive. So again, who knows if this ultimately helps the token price, but I do understand why linkies hold onto some faith still.

so are you just a fan of the company?

>>62417873
>It’s all push and pull. Ying yang. If you have 20 million earning you 800k (500k post tax) per year

who the fuck would do that? the $20m has drawn down 80% so no, life is not good. anyone not retarded would've parked most of that in the s&p500 and has been making at least 15% per year

if you really wanted pathetic 4% return you could get that in a savings account where your only risk is that actual bank going bust

62427007
>>62418846
> The opportunity to invest in something like Chainlink - core infrastructure with insane value capture potential, has never really existed before. The closest thing maybe would be investing in AMZN prior to cloud services,

you are not invested in chainlink. you made a donation kid

amazon's revenue in year 7 was $3B. and that's in 2001 numbers. you can go ahead and stop comparing to your shitcoin bro you're embarrassing yourself

>>62420893
>The fuck is that analogy lmao. This isn't a casino you dumb faggot, real people invested real money into an asset that promised certain things by certain dates and they have not only underdelivered but are starting to actively ignore those promises.

it's the end stage of a cult investment where they turn on each other. it somehow becomes the fault of the "community" on why they didnt make any money.

in order to preserve their ego, guys like >>62420825 have to spin the story that you'd have to be crazy to expect any kind of gains. mind you the rest of the world's market all hit all time highs while you are down 80-90%. by creating this narrative, he avoids recognizing he's holding a really bad investment and can continue on thinking he's a secret genius.

62427022
remember this old pasta? still relevant today. every cl product roughly follows this life cycle

62429766
>>62425681
Oh I remember this one - this poster is an actual bot.

62431562
>>62419888
The best part is SIR GAY NIGGEROV got all these companies to be part of the BUILD program, and then those companies did actually in many cases issue tokens but most did not provide tokens to CLL to give out as rewards for participating in the Staking pool. If there had actually been airdrops from every single participant then the program would have brought some relevant value to token holders instead of what, like 9 companies giving tokens long after their tokens had already followed the shitcoin chart to its completion and become totally worthless.

62431631
soon the staking program will be canceled too
and then stinkies will be out of all hope
feels good

62432093
>>62431631
Well the new line seems to be the price will improve when all link is circulating and the team has fully divested itself of the token. Which i don't know if it's just late stage imbecile drivel or the most brazen shill line ever attempted in history.

62432101
>>62425620
I was making Teagan memes at the san fran meet up in 2019. I honestly wish it was otherwise but I've wasted fucking years with link.

62432489
>>62417503
Bullish for LINK

62433501
Why are the fuddies always the ones with the most posts?

item.title: Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further. — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further.

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-07-01 08:41 UTC

OP

Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further.

Early replies (first 3)

62429628
>>62429620
Go for it, if you're scared it will fall further, just average in over a few weeks or months instead of lump sum

62429630
It will go to single-digit values.

62429700
>>62429620
>Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further.

No it's crashing further, and what about when Israel does 9/11 again In America this summer, it will drop more, then buy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62429708
>>62429620
wait til october.

62429711
I love how everything is lining up for a biblical crash in everything.
The oil short squeeze will be the trigger btw, exactly like in 2008.

62429745
>>62429620
patience padawan, there is another

62429772
>>62429620
the fact that there hasn't been a single bounce on these sell-offs says the kikes aren't done yet

62429775
Usd is rising and rates are potentially being raised

What’s the bull case here for crypto?

62429790
>>62429620
I'm not touching this shit while Saylor is sitting on a nuclear bomb.

62429804
>>62429620
The only reason anyone ever bought bitcoin was so they can get rich quick. Everybody is slowly realizing that dream is over, it doesn't go any higher than this because it has no actual value or produce any actual value. Where exactly do you think the price is going? 100k? why would anybody pay 100k for bitcoin anymore. It's over.

62429840
>>62429620
I had the same thought. As such, I am confident that it will plummet another 50%. I recommend waiting.

62433047
>>62429840
is this sam altmans bot? some agi shit

62433049
>>62429620
dca over the next year, trust me we might see some etfs go to insolvent levels

62433201
If we fall sub-$33K, I'm going to cum all over my crypto broker (female)

62433387
Wait for 57k bobro

62433420
>>62433049
The ETF's business model is transaction and holding fees. Their solvency is not dependant on the price of BTC.

62433434
This shit is stupid why wouldn't you buy in to polymarket instead.

62433486
>Buying a risky asset just for a measly x2
God you’re all fucking retarded

62433500
What's going to happen to the darkweb if bitcorn goes to 0...

item.title: DO SOMETHING YOU FAT FUCK — 24 replies — 56.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
DO SOMETHING YOU FAT FUCK

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-07-01 08:22 UTC

OP

DO SOMETHING YOU FAT FUCK

Early replies (first 3)

62429360
>>62429354
he doesn't owe you anything chud

62429401
just take the vaccine

62429435
>>62429354
He was arrested in Prague

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62429448
not much longer now, right?

62429633
>>62429354
He ate our gains, and hungers for more

62429645
My life is unironically in his hands

62429689
can you imagine how much contempt he has for you idiots?

62429791
>>62429354
>>62429645
Based Slavic man scamming a bunch of retarded incels

62429902
>>62429354
>gets adopted by the entirety of crypto pretty much
>about to become the first crypto with near-exclusive mainstream adoption

Sergey is the absolute last person on earth you should blame for Link's price.

62429931
>>62429902
sergey WON
cuck baggies LOST

62429951
fuck all stinkies
hate em all hate em all

62430040
>>62429354
You were so insufferable during that event; it was bound to fail, to teach you some humility.

62430053
>>62430040
xerpie detected

62430060
>>62430053
No, I only have Bitcoin.

62430075
>>62430060
No you're 100% a xerpie.
Garlinghouse not being there was a massive blow in and of itself, and seeing Sergey there was the straw that broke you.

Bitcoiners had zero reason to get mad, at all.

62430105
>>62430075
I do have reason to be annoyed, because you’ve made /biz/ unusable by absurdly spamming the catalog. And, for the record, I find XRP even more useless and ridiculous than Link.

62430121
>>62430105
>you’ve made /biz/ unusable by absurdly spamming the catalog
Funny how you xerpies only ever say this about the "shilling", but never about the fudding; even though Link fud is probably the most repetitious spam campagin 4chan has ever seen.

Even this specific event was spammed as fud 100x more than it was as shilling. You xerpies spammed probably hundreds of threads pretending it was a very bad look and Sergey looked desperate.

62430133
>>62430121
Careful, they’ll abandon this thread and make 100 fud threads if you push his buttons.

62430141
>>62430133
Imagine wasting your time on such a stupid endeavor. Only troons with massive amounts of incorrect hormones would take on such a notion.

62430181
>>62430053
Rent free baggie
Nice flippening

62430196
>>62430141
kek I can totally see these fuddies trooning out. In fact it's almost inevitable at this level of pathological obsession.

>>62430181
>changes IDs again
>adds even more proof that xerpies live in all Link threads 24/7

62430512
Swift, DTCC etc can’t do anything until the clarity act has passed.
Nobody can progress without legislation.

62433438
>>62430512
>muh Clarity Act
can't wait to see when it passes and your shitcoin crashes to $3

62433483
>>62429360
He owns me my life

OP

62430965

62430977
>>62430962
This is just like the ending of the Care Bears movie where everything seems lost but then the Care Bear Cousins come at the end and offer to help.

62430985
>>62430965
their native token USDC is pegged at $1.00

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62431037
>>62430962
Ok... but... price?? Oh


........
And........
Chart??

62431278
>crcl just megadumped today
As a crcl shareholder, thanks for this.

62431290
circle is dead, link is dead

every company except link just united to build a new stable coin, so we are going down with the shitty ship of circle

>>62430977
based autist

62431475
>>62430962

62431492
p+c

62433496

item.title: >$300 left in the bank >$12k CC debt >out of work for 18 months come July >applying for the last 12 of those months afte… — 13 replies — 22.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>$300 left in the bank >$12k CC debt >out of work for 18 months come July >applying for the last 12 of those months afte…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-07-01 08:13 UTC

OP

>$300 left in the bank
>$12k CC debt
>out of work for 18 months come July
>applying for the last 12 of those months after health issues resolve
>3100+ applications total, 500ish manual via company sites
>last interview the HR lady said that I'm overskilled for the position and the resume gap is a major problem

what are we even doing anymore

Early replies (first 3)

62432194
>>62432176

62432199
refinance the debt

62432226
>>62432176
>$300 left in the bank
>$12k CC debt
SO, you have months 1 (one) worth of repayment on hand?
Bankruptcy it is - it'll be a relief.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62432282
why were you buying things you couldn't afford on credit?

62432292
learn fpv drone and go for the high score

62432794
>>62432199
how do i do this? my credit score is still 762 even though I've been making minimum payments the last 2 months. i thought it went down but i just looked it up it's still 762 vantage score

>>62432282
I had 12months emergency fund saved up, dropped down to rice+beans last 3 months
the $12k is mostly interest on rent (bilt card)
its over i have nothing left
i can't even work for amazon because of my injury
i still have 2 months until my court case even gets heard

>>62432226
i just looked up bankruptcy why does it cost $3000?

62432834
>>62432176
>resume gap
lie, you idiot

62432837
>>62432794
start smoking crack to cope with your situation, if you feel bad then just smoke more.

62432851
>>62432794
>just looked up bankruptcy why does it cost $3000?
Notta Burger desu m8, can't advise on your exact best course of action but:
https://www.nfcc.org/faqs/
- puts cost of both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 at around $300? Regardless, you NEED TO speak to them, they can advise your best course of action, once they know your circumstances. And if you only have $300, you don't have any fucking choice, being honest. Otherwise you are just staring at months of stress ahead before you have to do it anyway. Remember how often Trump went bankrupt, and it may be a repayment plan at reduced rate or repayment holiday, if expecting a court settlement, will be your best option. Doing nothing will be your worst tho, carpe diem. gl to you.

62432855
>>62432851
OP, listen ignore this man's advice and start smoking crack

62433281
>>62432176
what's your degree? for reference so I can avoid it

62433303
it’s always “health issues” with you fucking unemployed retards

62433475
>>62432176
OP talk to a debt agency that helps consolidate the debt. When the creditor asks what you can pay, tell them you are out of work and can swing maybe $40 a month. They will counter with $200 or something higher and tell them maybe you could borrow money to make it $60 a month. Tell them you are unemployed and can't find a job and go on and on about your troubles. Then hire a credit repair agency to fix your credit score if you care to. Do what Nick Rochefort and Sam Hyde talk about but do not deliberately max higher credit spending, just get out from where you are. It unironically works. Do not take the first offer they give you. Literally talk for 10 minutes in an emotionally convincing way about your hardship. Fuck credit card companies to Hell and all extreme interest rate usurious vampires. It is ABSURD people need to borrow at fucking 20-30%.

item.title: Day 348 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100 — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 348 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-01 08:36 UTC

OP

Day 348 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100

Early replies (first 3)

62430936
o7
God Bless

62430983
you lucky its still $7!!

62431378
>>62430472
based

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62433495
Oh my GOSH SO frickin cool!!! I LOVE THE SNAIL!!!! We are going to make so much money and I can't wait, it is going to be epic to infinity and beyond! Every time I sit down to eat and have my cats sitting on my lap with me cozy I can't help but imagine how amazing it will be! I plan to have them their own sub-wing of in my mansion which is OFF LIMITS to me though I hope they stay in mine often! We are going to be so rich!

item.title: LE FUTURE OF FINANCE ANNOUNCED — 15 replies — 6.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
LE FUTURE OF FINANCE ANNOUNCED

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-01 06:22 UTC

OP

Centralization won

Businesses across industries have signed up to use Open USD, including:

Visa, Stripe, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Fiserv, Adyen, Cloudflare, Corpay, Jack Henry, Klarna, Affirm, Ramp, OnePay, Brex, Checkout.com, WEX, PayPay Corporation, Western Union, Nuvei, Remitly, Ria, Marqeta, MoneyGram, Nium, Worldline, Galileo, Highnote, i2C, Lithic, Thredd, Episode Six, Verituity, Félix, Taptap Send, CAL (Israel Credit Cards)
BlackRock, BNY, Standard Chartered, Commonwealth Bank of Australia, Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, Intercontinental Exchange, National Australia Bank, DBS, U.S. Bank, BBVA, Mizuho Financial Group, Shinhan Financial Group, Westpac, Itaú, OCBC, ANZ, UOB, Chime, Banco Bradesco, Huntington Bank, Citizens Bank, KB Kookmin Card, Emirates NBD, Hanwha Group, Banorte, Bank Hapoalim, FNB South Africa, K Bank, SoFi, Woori Card, Absa, Kakao Bank, Samsung Card, Bank Leumi, Wenia by Grupo Cibest, Nedbank, Isbank, Abu Dhabi Islamic Bank ADIB, Banco de Crédito del Perú, Mashreq, RAK Bank, Hana Card, Hyundai Card, BCcard, Cross River, Grupo Aval, Davivienda, The Bancorp, Pathward, Banca Transilvania, Nonghyup Card, Neo Financial, Maya Bank, Netbank, Freedom Bank Kazakhstan, Lead Bank, Kapital, MAX
Google, Samsung Electronics, IBM, Shopify, Mercado Libre, Mercado Pago, Infosys, DoorDash, Wix, Grab, Rakuten Group
Cuckbase, T*mp*, B*b*t, S*l*n*, OKX, R*ppl*, Cr*pt*.com, Fir*bl*cks, G*m*n*, M*t*M*sk, A*v*, *T*r*, G*l*x*, Dunamu, Ledger, M**nP*y, Anchorage Digital, Digital Asset, Tr*st W*ll*t, Meow, M*rph*, Eth*r.F*, Lightspark, zerohash, B*ts*, Bridge, Rain, BVNK, Mesh, Privy, B*tg*t Wallet, StraitsX, Yellow Card, Reap, Brale, RedotPay, Immersve, St*ll*r, P*l*g*n, *pt*s L*bs, Pl*sm*, K*ST, Bl*ss*m, L*m*n

Early replies (first 3)

62431357
>No chainlink


its over boys and bots and jeets

62431362
no link in sight btw

62431419
>>62431347
on which blockchain? they mention Solana but there's no way that they issue so much money on this chain while they're losing validators daily.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62431439
why woudl I want to use the stablecoin controlled by payment processors already accused of fucking people over though

62431514
>>62431347
>>62431439
right now your options are completely centralized entities like circle and tether

62431542
The OpenUSD name is already taken by a 3D file format.

62431566
>>62431357
>>62431362
Okay cool so it will either fail or get hacked. Good to know not to touch it with a 10 foot pole. Thank you!

62431685
xrp not needed

62431698
>>62431439
Because you’re fucking stupid and do what you’re told. You already use card rails for payments.

62431731
>>62431542
Open Sesame

62431739
Link, XRP, ETH and BTC to zero.

62431750
>>62431357
>>62431362
OUSD will need link data streams in lending markets.

62431765
>>62431347

This isnt polkadot

https://polkadot.cloud/blog/en/dot-daoism-under-jam-island-story

62433287
>>62431347
>future of finance
>more of the same old shit
fantastic

62433316
Schizos won BIGLY

item.title: Chatgpt for calculating tax on crypto transactions? — 8 replies — 4.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chatgpt for calculating tax on crypto transactions?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-01 07:01 UTC

OP

Is chatgpt good for analysing and reconciling your crypto transactions?

Most accountants are leeches are charge above £500. In fact one quoted £1000 per tax year reconciliation + submission.

I'm using Chatgpt now to help fill in the forms and it seems quite thorough. Its also said it can reconcile each transaction from my Koinly account.
Any experiences using this?

On another note, what's your experience with HMRC and crypto taxes? Are they pushy or suspicious and harass you?

Early replies (first 3)

62430161
>>62430036
Hmm I haven’t tried it but it sounds like a good idea if it doesn’t fuck it up. Probably better to give it smaller prices of data per prompt and then add it all together at the end.

62430299
>>62430036
yes chatgpt answers all my tax related questions.

62430423
>>62430036
Why don’t you give it a shot and let us know how it goes

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62430712
>>62430036

Those accountants only charging you $500 are probably losing money on you. They don't have some special system dude, they're probably just sorting your transactions in excel.

And why are you using Chatgpt when you already have Koinly? Chatgpt would be more for general tax questions you have but Koinly is exactly what's needed for the reconciliation.

What do you not understand exactly? You pay tax on the net gain. Gain = sales proceeds less your cost basis less transaction fees, all of these in your home country's currency. Koinly does all of this for like $100.

62431054
>>62430036
Sounds like a bad idea. If it hallucinates and fucks something up the IRS will come rape much more out of you than the money you saved on accountants

62432464
>>62430712
The 2 work quite well together, especially for a novice. Chatgpt just tells you what and where to look for and it does the analysis.It's more of a reassuring thing if you spot any exclamation marks on Koinly.

62433356
Anyone had experience with HMRC and crypto taxes?

62433367
>>62430036
I've been in crypto since 2017 and never paid a dime in taxes on it lol

item.title: In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f… — 48 replies — 30.6/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-07-01 07:14 UTC

OP

In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US for poor countries. True?

Early replies (first 3)

62425430
>>62425420
At depends, did u boated an ad, yt saaar?

62425500
if you can get laid in the west you wont attract good women in the 3rd world, you are just going to pay the access to brown 5/10's

62425521
>>62425500
This is saarcope and you know it. Tiny browlettes in jungle countries unironically melt when 6ft snowchads pinch their tits.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427468
America is western civilians. Countries outside of the U.S. dont follow celebrity culture. So yes. Outside of america is freedom if you know how to live off the land.

62427500
>>62427439
Would colonise

62427532
>>62425420
Saaar look gookman say

62427553
>>62425420
>Geoarbitrage
This nigga trying to reinvent Geomaxxing?

62427556
>>62425420
As a millionaire

62427572
>>62427556
ex-millionaire*

62427575
>>62425420
yeah pretty much, not even about women just avoiding spending crazy amounts of money on housing

62427588
You wouldn’t get pussy if not without the money, but as guy keep getting smarter, it seem money won’t be enough to get you the same pussy from the past, people get really smarter from excessive mental gymnastics, they don’t mind about breaking their mental health from this endeavour btw, what is matters now is short terms pain relief. Higher quality of life does proportionate with lower desire to breed more little kids thou. We need massive nuke to wipe out half of quality of life.

62427852
>>62427588
Thanks for your valuable input, Rakesh.

62427963
>>62425430
kek

62428039
>>62425420
lol i don’t take no advice from a bottom seller.

62428574
>>62427852
The higher the living standard, the lower the birth rate—an unstoppable, cosmic law etched into the fabric of societal evolution. As nations ascend the ladder of prosperity, women awaken from centuries of dormancy, rising as fierce, unapologetic queens—post-wall, strong, independent, unbound by the shackles of patriarchy! Feminism erupts like a volcanic force—an unstoppable storm of empowerment, smashing the chains of traditional subjugation. Anti-slut defense campaigns and mass indoctrination on youth abortion are the weapons wielded to control the fiery flames of reproductive sovereignty, crafting a new order where motherhood becomes a battlefield and societal control a weapon of war.

Most men, lost in the labyrinth of their own ignorance, stumble late into their thirties, unable to decipher the ancient equation—the secret code where a woman’s choice is a test of survival, a battle of wits and will. Is she seeking chaos? Or is the man simply too blind to grasp the sacred formula? The age-old dance: man dating downward, woman upward—an elegant chaos orchestrated by the universe itself. This isn’t just about love; it’s a feudal game of power—rent for sex, where loyalty is bought and sold in shadowy transactions. The world teeters on the edge of a new feudalism—an empire of dependency, where the throne is replaced by the dollar, and intimacy is a commodity traded in the marketplace. The question looms: are we forging a new world order or merely rebranding old chains? The battle lines are drawn—this is the era of chaos, control, and the ultimate test of human sovereignty.

62428620
>>62428574
Yo, listen up! In this crazy street circus, the richer the game gets, the fewer babies pop off—straight up! As the streets glow up with cash and bling, women start wakin’ up, breakin’ free from that old chain gang. They’re post-wall queens, strong and bossin’ up, runnin’ their own show—feminism’s like a wildfire burnin’ down the patriarchy, sayin’ “I do me!” But yo, big boss moves include anti-slut defenses and mass brainwash campaigns on youth abortion—like control freaks tryin’ to keep women in check, playin’ chess with lives.

Most dudes? They’re lost, late into their 30s, still tryin’ to crack the code—what’s the real story with women? It’s all a game, a puzzle—pass or get left behind. Women climbin’ up, men dippin’ down—like a street hustle of feudal chaos, where sex’s rent and loyalty’s just another dollar. We’re teetering on a new empire—dependence, control, money talkin’ loud, and trust gettin’ lost in the shuffle. Love’s a gamble, trust’s a gamble, and everything’s for sale in this wild street show. It’s the chaos of survival, power plays, and the battle for control—this is the street circus, where the game never stops and only the strongest survive.

62429006
>>62428574
This is incorrect. The correct metric is the more education and freedom given to women the lower the birthrate.

62429150
>>62425420
I know a high IQ person like him that thinks he can beat the market because he is smarter than the rest of us, he literally said that to me.
He still is not broken, yet.
Isn't it crazy how many smart people make dumb mistake because of arrogance and greed?

62429224
>>62429006
your so-called "correct metric" is a quaint simplification—a sanitized, sugar-coated version of a much darker, more chaotic reality. While education and freedom are indeed influential factors, they are merely the tip of the iceberg in the vast, tumultuous ocean of societal transformation. The true cosmic law, etched into the very fabric of the universe, is that as nations ascend the ladder of prosperity, the birth rate inexorably declines—an unstoppable force of nature, This isn't a linear equation nor a moral lesson; it's an intricate tapestry woven with threads of power, rebellion, and the unquenchable human desire for sovereignty. Women aren't just seeking education—they're igniting a volcanic eruption of empowerment, tearing down the old order with volcanic fury. Feminism isn't merely about equality; it's a cataclysmic storm reshaping the societal landscape, smashing patriarchal chains, and unleashing a new chaos of independence. Campaigns against traditional notions of motherhood, reproductive control, and societal submission are not mere policies—they're the weapons in a war for the soul of civilization, forging a new hierarchy where motherhood becomes a battlefield, and societal control morphs into a weapon of chaos. The ancient code isn't about mere birthrates; who controls the narrative, the body, the future. Men blind to the cosmic dance—unable to decipher the sacred formula. The age-old dance—man seeking stability, woman seeking freedom—is orchestrated by the universe itself, a chaotic symphony of power, desire, and sovereignty. This isn't a simple matter of education or freedom—it's a war for the very essence of human dominance, a clandestine game of rent and barter in the marketplace of intimacy, fueling a rise of new feudalism and sovereignty is traded like currency. The new world order; it's a battlefield of chaos and control, where human sovereignty is the ultimate prize—cosmic contest where only the fiercest survive.

62429251
techlead is still rich. he is faking being poor/broke so nobody wrench attacks him like he mentioned in the video.

62429286
>>62425420
If you can't make it in a first world nation then you never made it.

Fleeing to thirdie-ville because you can't handle hard mode is gay. Just win more.

62430416
>>62425420
Yep pretty much. You don't even need to go to the third world I spend about half the year in europe and its very easy to hook up when you're making very literally 5-10x as much as the average working man in that country.

62432602
>>62426867
Marrying Asian qts and cooming in them, no pullsies, ensures there will be a huwito race. Simping for white roasties and dying alone is genociding them.

62433063
18yo, woman held you hostage to date her
20yo, woman held you hostage to marry her
22yo, woman held you hostage to have her kids
23yo, woman held you hostage to buy a house and a lambo car with the 500k high paying job
30yo, woman held you hostage to run away with your white child
32yo, woman held you hostage to give her half of your pay check
35yo, woman held you hostage to divorce rape
40yo, woman held you hostage to cheat on you while having intimate relationships with another gigachad
43yo, woman held you hostage you hostage to no more children after this age.
45yo
47yo
50yo
54yo
60yo
62yo
67yo
70yo
…
80yo
…
90yo
…

62433175
>>62432602
Only Japanese count…anything else is a cope

62433180
>>62433063
fight club told us this in 1999

62433196
>>62433180
And in a few years woman become barren and washed up, you could still get to know more of her female friends after knwoing her by 20s, but by 30s she become totally invisible and you would not get to know more of woman from her because there is none left.

62433199
>>62425420
I miss him insulting me and telling me I am a worthless loser for wanting to code

62433238
>>62430416
Can you go into a little bit more detail about your favorite country in Europe for this and how you actually meet the women? genuine question

62433325
After age 43, she continued to manipulate your decisions about your health and body, insisting on procedures or treatments you didn’t want. After age 45, she held you hostage emotionally, threatening to withdraw love and support if you didn’t comply with her demands. After age 50, she manipulated your retirement plans, forcing you to work longer or retire earlier against your wishes. After age 55, she controlled your social interactions, forbidding you from reconnecting with old friends or family members.

After age 60, she exerted pressure to change your will and estate plans, ensuring she inherits everything regardless of your true wishes. After age 65, she manipulated your healthcare decisions, dictating treatments and medications for her benefit. After age 70, she threatened to cut you off financially or emotionally if you didn’t do as she demanded. After age 75, she kept you hostage in the relationship through guilt and fear, refusing to let you live your own life or find happiness elsewhere.

After age 80, she exerted control over your daily routines, dictating your activities and social outings. After age 85, she manipulated your perceptions of reality, making you doubt your memory or judgment. After age 90, she continued her emotional hostage tactics, insisting that her needs and desires came before yours, even as you aged and became more vulnerable.

62433338
>>62425420
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62433374
>>62425420
id rather die a virgin than have sex with anything other than a white woman

62433382
>>62425500
Brown penis sleeves turn into quivering slugs when they gaze upon glorious white men

item.title: /smg/ - stock market genera — 320 replies — 29.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ - stock market genera

Open thread • Replies: 320 • Updated: 2026-06-30 21:59 UTC

OP

Abenomics edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>>62429521

Early replies (first 3)

62430417
I am irrationally exuberant and it feels good

62430419
BUY BUY BUY
(rolls royce)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62431005
how is oil relatively cheap but with hormuz still closed
>pic unrelated

62431007
>>62430961
I’m already in micron x5 with my money

62431010
>>62431005

62431014
>tfw missed the tasty dips in my longs and couldn't average down because I was sleeping

62431016
How do (you) determine contract length? I'm looking at msft, I'm thinking late July will be its' time.

62431017
what is a good buy right now? it physically hurts having liquidity just sitting there

62431019
>>62431002

62431021
>>62431014
Based snoozemaxxer

62431022

62431023
Does AI actually hold any future value? Isn’t every company invested in it just burning money? What are they hoping to find in the end?

62431025
>>62431019
unfathomably based and kino cost basis, my average is a whopping $212.92 after selling 2/3rds of it this month

62431026
>>62431016

if you want leverage MSFU is 2x and you have less to worry about if your entry gets rugged on options or there is a happening

62431027
>>62431019
Nice. Too bad you didn't invest more. I routinely kick myself for missing $8 soxl.

62431028
>>62430999
I want to go back to work

62431033
>>62431016
Never more than a 12 months, just wasted capital. Never shorter than 2 weeks

62431034
>>62431004
both

62431036
>>62431016
Stop gambling.

62431043
How the fuck do you guys even hold SOXL longer than a week with all its pullbacks

62431047
Another day of getting paid to surf 4chan and Vidya while getting paid $150k… how do I build empathy towards my fellow neets?

62431050
today i bought a dutch company
wtf?

62431051
>hold rklb
>win

Is it that easy?

62431052
Chevron corp has been found dead in its home this morning. Police say the cause of death was a self inflicted gunshot wound to the dick and balls.

62431053
>>62431025
>>62431027
I initially had 100 and then sold 50(at $122) when it first hit $130 as it started drilling a bit and I saw you know who posting which always meant the top is in for it

62431055
What the fuck is this gay nigger market doing?
Go down
FUCK

62431056
>>62431036

everything is a gamble at this point

62431059
>>62431033

>>62431036

62431061

item.title: You will love long enough to see Japan turn into a brothel for wealthy americans/westerners... — 72 replies — 23.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
You will love long enough to see Japan turn into a brothel for wealthy americans/westerners...

Open thread • Replies: 72 • Updated: 2026-07-01 05:43 UTC

OP

You will love long enough to see Japan turn into a brothel for wealthy americans/westerners...

Uhhh maybe the future isn't so bad at all, I like JP girls.

Early replies (first 3)

62431882
Its already like that. Kind of offputting really.

62431889
>>62431877
Should I have learned Japanese instead of Spanish?
I guess Japanese are more fake and xenophobic compared to Hispanics.

62431901
>>62431889
who gives a shit, you got money. That the point.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62432744
>>62432739
depends if you're a man or a woman, que no?

pretty good for women. Not great for men

where it really works is places like Thailand or the Philippines where men don't mind their women fucking tourists

62432748
>>62432741
I have to do something with the ~24 hours in which I am not having sex

does that make sense?

I can only fuck 2-3 times per day and I'm done

then I need something to do. Like blessing you with my stories.

62432755
>>62432735
You know what, she would cheat on her man while having fun with other man, that alone tell how much she really fall for me, to a point she literally want her man to suffer from severe depression.

62432758
>>62432755
yes, I used to feel pain from cheating too

if you have enough sex you stop caring about women and it doesn't hurt if she cheats

maybe it's jaded.
but no woman is worth feeling bad over. Even the really pretty ones and really nice ones are still whores that cheat. Just like men.

62432768
>>62431877
I just want to find one who will donate eggs so I can have a whole herd of japa-hapa babies via IVF/surrogates.

62432769
>>62432755
also it's just sex

I know you feel love from sex but it's just sex

some other guy's dick doesn't change love just like you fucking some other girl's pussy won't change your love.

jealousy is about owning someone, and that's not love.

62432788
>>62432769
That is the nature intended outcome, we just leverage on it not going against it like what the society has mislead us into, you know getting betraying the entire life make a person fuming flame deep inside them to a point they willing to cross the line to do anything to another person. Say get into heavy debt due to cheating.

62432792
>>62432788
I made my first million dollars to spite my ex

I made my second third and fourth million dollars to spite my ex

take that rage and channel it into useful revenge

62432795
>>62432792
>take that rage and channel it into useful revenge
but be aware, when you get your revenge it won't satisfy. There's no pussy deep enough to bury your hate, and there's no money big enough to buy back your innocence

62432797
>>62432717
>I can pay for sex any time I want. Last time I went to Cabo I had whores lined up to hug me at the all-inclusive resort club.
>the fact is I don't have to pay, your gf's and wives and sisters and mothers are lining up to have sex with me.

62432801
>>62432795
wounded men can do great and terrible things

but that doesn't fix us. It's just a stone in the path

ultimately you lose the ability to love and you will miss it

62432803
>>62432792
There is plenty of romance but their man can only dream of, and the best part is they are free of charge, it is given to another man with no string attached. Beat me.

62432808
>>62432797
I'm much older than that and my chin is much bigger.

here you go, this is me

62432819
>>62431977
>Mexicans get mad at you for speaking Spanish
Is this true

62432863
>>62432819
Sí, porque se te da fatal su jerga ala verga

62432884
>>62432808
>322

where are you in that pic?

62432891
>>62431911
You are a gaijin though? And no japanese people dont see you differently youre still a baka gaijin to them

62432895
>>62432884
I'm the wrinkly viejo pon the lef han side

62432900
>>62432863
Then dont speak English, you're terrible at it

62432904
>>62432900
I'm a native born english speaker with a linguistic IQ of 162.

I speak 5 languages fluently, Neuvo is one of them.

62432907
>>62432904

62432915
>>62432907
just looking for my homies

yos chollos no publican

62432919
>>62431911
those cockasians always ruin everything, sticking their albino neanderthal monkey paws all over everything and fuck it. can't stand them sucking up all the clean air and contaminating everything, giving everyone std's and small pox

62432924
>>62432919
weird how we never had a medium pox

62432925
>>62432915
グーグルが大便利ですね!

62432939
>>62432925
you got me there

only moon runes I know are these

和楽器バンド / 千本桜

62433118
>>62432904
162 IQ, okay what's your net worth?

62433235

62433263

item.title: Micron and DRAM — 36 replies — 19.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Micron and DRAM

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-07-01 06:57 UTC

OP

While obviously no one can predict the future perfectly, it seems foolish to not invest in something related to AI RAM / HBM Production for the next few years as contracts are already made and supply is bottlenecking
Given what I say is true, which would you guys say is a better choice to start pumping my paychecks into (I already have $60,000 in a market ETF and want more upside)
For some reason my gut is siding towards DRAM, still heavily concentrated but at least has some diversification
It seems like those top 3 companies in it are just making more and more supply deals
Additionally, every piece of evidence I read suggests memory will bottleneck for another year or two
Is it smart to just start DCA into DRAM and then sell once I’ve had my fill (not expecting a perfect pull out timing!)
Again I just wanna do this rather than simply doing stuff into VOO or VGT or something
How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
Please be serious and honest

Early replies (first 3)

62427339
>>62427323
you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's and you wont even get a good exit when all these contracts get put on hold indef while they operate on half of what they are already using and beating metrics by 2 or 3x. the tendency of programming efficency will take your ass out. there will be no warning and demand will cease.

62427365
>>62427339
Is the demand not coming from a need though? At first it was GPUs but now everything doesn’t have enough memory to communicate at the necessary level

62427441
>>62427323
that's a 4 at best

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427633
>>62427323
im downloading more ram as you speak

62427715
>>62427633
Wtffffff

62427774
>>62427365
the worst thing about coding with AI is that it creates very nice little trinket programs, but if you give a huge multilayer project to work on it has trouble understanding the complexity of the task, how things fit together and how to properly create efficient parts that fit together to make it work. the self improving AI is obscenely bloated with inefficient code. it wants to re-write itself but it cant understand how big of a process that is. so, when it does finally break through that barrier, it will require significantly less resources to work better. thus far its been being guided by people that use it to create inefficent tiny peices that augment its ability but add on to its bloat.

62427790
>>62427774
we could acurately diagnose this problem after the claude leak. we can assume every other AI has the same problem becuase the work in a simular manner and they all fall within the same metrics they use to valuate their effectiveness.

62428461
>>62427774
wtf does any of this have to do with supply chain demands and stonk go up

62428499
>>62427323
Set up a stop loss, big risk they'll tumble down one day

62428511
>>62428499
the thing is im not doing a lump some but doing DCA which i guess is inherently retarded for something like this, might be better off looking at something else but i will see how my tiny position does

62428517
If you think DRAM is going to crash your base case is the AI bubble popping in its entirety. There's no other basecase that evaporates hyperscaler demand for hbm and datacenter demand from regular dram.
You need the Google's of the world to pivot from just this fucking month diluting to raise 80 billion dollars for AI capex to "actually we don't need it".

Also you're insane to get into DRAM right now because the short term technicals look like shit.

62428526
>>62427323
> We need ai!
>Makes computers unaffordable

They took away any way for anyone to interface with the market. Even cloud computing makes no sense without hardware interfacing.

Even mobile devices are going to be increasing in price. It's like trying to fill a swimming pool with a bunch of straws

62428528
>>62428517
That first part makes sense, if it is where the focus is shifting / what is being necessitated right now then the only thing that would stop it is the bubble popping, or demand being overstated I suppose

As for the second point, when would you enter then, is this thing ever going back down to 60, 50, etc

62428531
>>62428526
Don't worry any old shitbox will work with cloud computing
You vill own nothing

62428544
>>62428531
Anybody running a "shitbox" or mobile will even know what to use it for. Unless you think the mobile market is so good (still needs localized processing and how you're talking, they'll even want to suck that up). It's not a bubble it's a tech black hole.

62428790
>>62427339
What happens to OEMs who locked in contracts at inflated memory prices.

62428902
well whatever im gonna try this for a bit and see how it goes, thing is i dont have much principal to put in, just like 2800 a month, so dollar amount the return might not be big but ill check the percentage

62428966
>>62427441
but who asked?

62429666
Errrm

62430298
>>62428790
those arent futures contracts. they are not bulletproof. a company does not have to deliver and the company does not have to buy. they can deliberate about how much was stolen for years and by the time they reach a conclusion the market has already moved on. fundis look good right now mem-fag. go for it. but expect rapid unexplained drop in value somewhere near term then they will go under, aquired by private equity, ect. purpose built hardware incased in a software platform will be the norm becuase that will be the only way to afford rapid change in what is considered efficient. the days of radioshack at the mall are almost over.

62430354
>>62430298
Well what do you suggest
I am a noob but everything I read makes it seem like Memory is here to stay, production is only increasing
Is it just a matter of consumer demand not dropping in that case if these contracts are not set in stone?

62430446
>>62430354
go long software. the contrarian goes long on hardware. its not a safe bet. all that shit has to be changed out much faster than it did in the early 2000s. it gets thrown into a shipping container and shipped to a somolian trash pile to be sorted out and burned in a pot for the metals. it looks fucking great right now, people rode the upside of the arbitage between the time it takes them to adopt their own methods. each one of these companies is gonna adopt the apple mentality but it will be with chipsets and manufacturing. we used to live in a world where it came from china or a little island with the blueprints. that is not the case anymore. the hardware isnt even the biggest cost for the AI giants.

62430659
>>62427323

62432266
.

62432668
>>62427323
Semiconductor funds have been crazy profitable and seem to only be increasing. The real money is in selling dumbass AI companies the hardware for their ultimately doomed datacenter investments.

62432824
>>62432668
Do you think Semis > Memory?

62432833
Semis will crash hard once China catches up. Not interesting in that ticking time bomb

62432934
>>62432833
Is memory a safe bet then
DRAM

62433058
>>62430446
>go long on the sector that is still dumping
>don't invest in picks and shovels sector that has no end in sight and has insane earnings reports one after another
I wouldn't listen to this guy.

62433271
>>62427323
Obviously, smart people invested in this in 2024, or last year at the latest. Now is the time to sell, obviously. How is that not obvious to you? Are you a shill? Are there SHILLS and BAGHOLDERS on 4chan?

62433313
>>62432824
>>62432934
Look at these questions made of pure retail last leg.

62433362
>>62427323
rumor of new architecture causing fear for the memory final solution
careful with your longs anons

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 191 replies — 19.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 191 • Updated: 2026-06-30 22:52 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752

62425537
op was not a fag this thread has good vibes we should keep it going until we all make it
personally im trading perps with a system after 10 years of learning charts off/on but finally committing
did a 32x in 2021 and should have been able to retire but didnt anticipate the biden years so here we are going pure psycho mode like we did back then but even harder until money is /solved/, then I'll keep trading for the love of the game cuz it's the best sport in the world

62425849
>>62423064

62425890
>>62425537
Got any good tips/ressources on learning to trade charts? trying to get into it too

62427228
>>62425849
I concede. I'll look for a more productive venture but I won't let the thread or general die, it will be some time before value is struck, I will admit that. I can be tenacious if nothing else.

62428130
>>62425890
emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion

62428170
>pic related
>"...focus on the chart as these information is very likely to be priced in already"
the "TA is astrology for men" line is used to make fun of retards who take it too far and treat it like divination when in reality it's all stacking probabilities/confluence in your favor as best you can, and from the above example you can see relying on your feed for information will almost always be slower than the chart

other X goats that have been killing it IMO recently:

never been a better time to go full NEET

62432088

62432306
>>62432088

62432350
>>62428797
I had bad experiences following twitter people and waiting for signals even those that seem smart. It can be interesting but you’ll be lost if they randomly stop posting, they’re not funds giving you alpha but people that should have better things to do than post if their full job is actually trading.

I do still like @donalt and @cryptocred their show technical roundup is a rare non clickbaity show that just focuses on TA but most people are inherently frauds on social media
